problem_statement: The payroll dashboard currently displays all employees in a flat, unfiltered list. When the employee count grows, or when a user wants to quickly locate a specific person or identify employees in a salary band, there is no way to narrow down the results without manually scanning the entire roster. Adding search and salary-range filters to the existing dashboard will make the employee directory more useful while demonstrating a full spec-driven OpenHands + OpenSpec feature workflow.

user_stories.i_want: to search employees by name or job title
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly locate a specific person or role in the roster

user_stories.i_want: to filter employees by a minimum and maximum salary range
user_stories.so_that: I can identify everyone who falls within a particular compensation band

user_stories.i_want: to see which filters are currently active and clear them in one click
user_stories.so_that: I can easily reset the view to the full employee list without refreshing the page

user_stories.i_want: to see search and filter controls added to the existing payroll dashboard without breaking CRUD operations
user_stories.so_that: I can evaluate how OpenSpec-driven development incrementally extends a working application

must_have: A text search input on the dashboard that filters the employee roster by name or job title (case-insensitive, partial match)
A minimum salary input that hides employees earning below the specified amount
A maximum salary input that hides employees earning above the specified amount
Active filter state is reflected in the UI so the user knows filtering is in effect
A clear/reset button that removes all active filters and restores the full employee list
Filtering applies to both the summary table and the expandable employee cards sections
All existing CRUD operations (add, update, delete employee) continue to work unchanged
Input sanitization: salary filter values must be non-negative integers; non-numeric input is ignored gracefully
should_have: Filter controls are visually co-located as a cohesive filter bar above or alongside the employee roster
An empty-state message is shown when no employees match the active filters (e.g., 'No employees match your search')
Filter state persists in the URL as query parameters (e.g., ?search=Sofia&minSalary=80000) so filtered views can be bookmarked or shared
The clear button is only visible when at least one filter is active
The active filter count or summary label (e.g., 'Showing 3 of 6 employees') is displayed in or near the roster heading
nice_to_have: Client-side live filtering without a full page reload (debounced input events)
Filter state preserved across page navigation within the same session
Salary histogram or range slider for visual salary exploration

acceptance_criteria: Entering a partial name (e.g., 'chen') in the search input shows only employees whose name or title contains that string (case-insensitive)
Entering a min salary of 100000 hides all employees with a salary below $100,000
Entering a max salary of 150000 hides all employees with a salary above $150,000
Applying both min and max salary filters shows only employees within the specified range
Combining a text search with salary filters narrows results using both criteria simultaneously
When filters are active, a visible indicator (badge, label, or highlighted state) communicates that results are filtered
Clicking the clear/reset button removes all filter values and restores the full unfiltered roster
When no employees match the active filters, a meaningful empty-state message is shown instead of a blank roster
Non-numeric values entered in the salary fields are ignored and do not cause errors
All add, update, and delete employee operations work correctly with or without active filters
The salary statistics in the summary cards (total payroll, average salary, etc.) always reflect all employees, not just the filtered set
out_of_scope: Filters applied to the /api/employees JSON endpoint (API responses always return all employees)
Filtering by manager, home address, or other fields beyond name, title, and salary
Authentication or permission-scoped filtering (e.g., hiding sensitive salary data from certain roles)
Changes to the database schema or existing data model
Any modifications to the existing employee CRUD forms or their submission logic
open_questions: Should filtering be implemented server-side (query parameters trigger a page reload and server re-queries SQLite) or client-side (JavaScript filters the DOM after the full page loads)? Server-side is consistent with the existing Express + redirect pattern; client-side avoids page reloads but adds JavaScript complexity.

Should the salary summary cards (total payroll, average salary, headcount) update to reflect only the filtered employees, or always reflect all employees regardless of filters? Reflecting all employees avoids confusion and requires no schema changes.
Should the min/max salary inputs use free-text number fields or a range slider widget? Free-text is simpler and consistent with the existing form style.
Is there a preferred visual placement for the filter bar — above the roster table, in the left sidebar alongside the add-employee form, or as a collapsible panel?
